# Plan Quality Checklist: PRD M1 — Slot-First Launch & Dry-Run

**Purpose**: Validate that created planning artifacts (`plan.md`, `research.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/`, `quickstart.md`) are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable.

**Created**: 2026-04-10  
**Feature**: `specs/001-prd-mvp-spec/spec.md`

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit.checklist` workflow; evaluates requirement quality (not implementation behavior).

---

## How to Use

- **PASS**: Checklist item is satisfied. Mark with `[x]`.
- **FAIL**: Checklist item is not satisfied. Mark with `[ ]`.
- **N/A**: Not applicable to this project phase. Mark with `[n]`.

Update this file after each planning iteration. All items MUST be PASS before merging spec changes.

---

## plan.md

- [ ] All requirements have unique IDs (e.g., `FR-001`, `NFR-001`)
- [ ] Each requirement has clear acceptance criteria
- [ ] Requirements are testable (no ambiguous terms like "fast", "user-friendly")
- [ ] Dependencies between requirements are documented
- [ ] Requirements cover both happy path and error cases
- [ ] Non-functional requirements (performance, security) are specified

## research.md

- [ ] Design decisions reference concrete alternatives considered
- [ ] Trade-offs are explicitly documented
- [ ] External references (RFCs, spec links) are valid and accessible
- [ ] Research findings directly inform requirement definitions
- [ ] Open questions are tracked with resolution status

## data-model.md

- [ ] All data structures have explicit field types
- [ ] Field constraints are documented (min, max, required, patterns)
- [ ] Relationships between entities are defined
- [ ] Validation rules are specified for each field
- [ ] Example payloads are provided for complex structures
- [ ] Migration strategies are addressed if applicable

## contracts/

- [ ] Each contract has clear purpose and scope
- [ ] Error contracts define all failure modes
- [ ] Success response schemas are fully specified
- [ ] Field naming conventions are consistent across contracts
- [ ] Versioning strategy is defined (if applicable)
- [ ] Contracts are testable (examples provided)

## quickstart.md

- [ ] Prerequisites are explicitly listed
- [ ] Installation steps are sequential and numbered
- [ ] All commands are copy-pasteable and tested
- [ ] Expected outputs are shown for each step
- [ ] Troubleshooting section covers common failures
- [ ] Links to deeper documentation are provided

---

**Status**: In progress | **Last Updated**: 2026-04-14
